jokes.text: I thought that was, like, a little comedy hat or something. That's their actual hair...
jokes.setup: Mac is disgusted by women's haircuts, building tension about their appearance
jokes.punchline: He genuinely thought their short hair was decorative hats
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac obliviousness - his complete inability to recognize hair styles is both absurd and character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I thought that was, like, a little comedy hat or something. That's their actual hair...

jokes.text: Poppins comes and goes as he pleases 'cause that's his right as a male. Women are never gonna get that.
jokes.setup: Mac is explaining his dog's behavior
jokes.punchline: He uses it to make a sexist generalization about gender roles
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac applying toxic masculinity to pet ownership - perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Poppins comes and goes as he pleases 'cause that's his right as a male. Women are never gonna get that.

jokes.text: If I want to have a baby and then abandon that baby, I have the right to do that.
jokes.setup: Dee is arguing for women's rights and bodily autonomy
jokes.punchline: She escalates to abandoning babies as her example of choice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark escalation where Dee's feminist argument goes to an unhinged extreme - shocking but earned

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: If I want to have a baby and then abandon that baby, I have the right to do that.

jokes.text: making themselves no longer sexually attractive to me, and that upsets the natural balance of things
jokes.setup: Dennis is critiquing women's haircut choices
jokes.punchline: He frames it as disrupting the natural order because HE finds them unattractive
jokes.explanation: Peak Dennis narcissism - believing his personal attraction affects cosmic balance

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: making themselves no longer sexually attractive to me, and that upsets the natural balance of things

jokes.text: I want to jump-start Poppins's system. You know? But it's not working. Maybe if I do it longer?
jokes.setup: Mac is trying to help his sick dog
jokes.punchline: His solution is blasting an air horn directly into the dog's ear
jokes.explanation: Mac's logic of 'air horn = medical treatment' is beautifully stupid

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I want to jump-start Poppins's system. You know? But it's not working. Maybe if I do it longer?

jokes.text: You blast him with the air horn, a-and that might wake him up. Oh, and maybe I'll scream into the other ear.
jokes.setup: Charlie is helping Mac's air horn 'treatment'
jokes.punchline: He adds screaming into the other ear as additional 'therapy'
jokes.explanation: Charlie naturally escalates Mac's bad idea into an even worse one

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You blast him with the air horn, a-and that might wake him up. Oh, and maybe I'll scream into the other ear.

jokes.text: If it's not, could you say it in that really funny Arnold Schwarzenegger voice that's, like, so funny? 'It's not a tumor.'
jokes.setup: Mac asks the vet about a tumor
jokes.punchline: He wants the vet to do an Arnold impression regardless of the diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Mac's inappropriate timing and obsession with pop culture impressions during a medical crisis

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: If it's not, could you say it in that really funny Arnold Schwarzenegger voice that's, like, so funny? 'It's not a tumor.'

jokes.text: Wait. Poppins is a girl?
jokes.setup: The vet reveals Poppins is pregnant
jokes.punchline: Mac has owned this dog for years without knowing its gender
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Mac's complete negligence as a pet owner revealed in one line

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Wait. Poppins is a girl?

jokes.text: Oh, nah, I-I could never get underneath that hind leg to check. You don't want to try to get under Poppins.
jokes.setup: Mac explains why he doesn't know his dog's gender
jokes.punchline: The dog is apparently so aggressive he can't even check its sex
jokes.explanation: Adds to the absurdity of Mac's relationship with this dangerous dog

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, nah, I-I could never get underneath that hind leg to check. You don't want to try to get under Poppins.

jokes.text: one time I tried to give him a belly scratch and he bit my thumbnail clean off
jokes.setup: Mac continues explaining Poppins's aggression
jokes.punchline: A simple belly rub resulted in digit amputation
jokes.explanation: Vivid, grotesque detail that escalates the dog's dangerousness

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: one time I tried to give him a belly scratch and he bit my thumbnail clean off

jokes.text: I can't believe that Poppins is gonna be a father. A mother. What? A mother? He is?
jokes.setup: Mac struggles with the gender revelation
jokes.punchline: He keeps switching between father/mother while using wrong pronouns
jokes.explanation: Mac's genuine confusion creates sustained awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I can't believe that Poppins is gonna be a father. A mother. What? A mother? He is?

jokes.text: we don't really do gender at our bar anymore. Yeah. Like, gender's so, like, old school.
jokes.setup: The vet questions if they understand gender
jokes.punchline: They claim to be progressive while demonstrating complete ignorance
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - they think they're woke while being completely clueless

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: we don't really do gender at our bar anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah. Like, gender's so, like, old school.

jokes.text: Well, you got to kill those babies, then. What? Dude, what the hell? I thought you were all pro-life and everything. I am, but this is different. This affects me.
jokes.setup: Mac learns his dog will die if she gives birth
jokes.punchline: His pro-life stance instantly reverses when it personally affects him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant hypocrisy reveal - Mac's principles crumble the instant they inconvenience him. Perfect character work.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, you got to kill those babies, then.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What? Dude, what the hell? I thought you were all pro-life and everything.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I am, but this is different. This affects me.

jokes.text: did you know that hair only grows about six inches over the course of an entire year?
jokes.setup: Dennis and Charlie are accosting a woman going to the salon
jokes.punchline: Dennis uses fake hair facts as his opening gambit
jokes.explanation: Dennis's creepy approach disguised as helpful information

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: did you know that hair only grows about six inches over the course of an entire year?

jokes.text: With this one tragic decision, Keri Russell alienated all of her fans, she got her show canceled, and she was never heard of ever again. Yeah, but Keri Russell works all the time.
jokes.setup: Dennis claims Keri Russell's haircut ruined her career
jokes.punchline: The woman immediately debunks his false claim
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Dennis's confident but completely wrong argument

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: With this one tragic decision, Keri Russell alienated all of her fans, she got her show canceled, and she was never heard of ever again.

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: Yeah, but Keri Russell works all the time.

jokes.text: Yeah, now, does she still play women? Probably in a cape or something.
jokes.setup: Dennis is challenged on his Keri Russell claim
jokes.punchline: He doubles down by suggesting she only plays caped characters now
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd response that shows Dennis won't admit he's wrong

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah, now, does she still play women? Probably in a cape or something.

jokes.text: You don't get a monkey cut like that unless you're broken inside.
jokes.setup: Dee is using a woman as an example of choice
jokes.punchline: She calls her haircut a 'monkey cut' while defending her
jokes.explanation: Dee's 'support' is more brutal than outright hostility

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You don't get a monkey cut like that unless you're broken inside.

jokes.text: Go full baboon, girl. I support you.
jokes.setup: Dee continues her 'supportive' animal comparisons
jokes.punchline: She escalates from monkey to baboon while claiming to be supportive
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that makes Dee's 'support' even worse. Great economy.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Go full baboon, girl. I support you.

jokes.text: men should have some say in what women do with their hair? I mean, for Christ's sakes, we're the ones that have to look at it all day!
jokes.setup: Dennis argues for men having input on women's choices
jokes.punchline: His reasoning is entirely about his own visual comfort
jokes.explanation: Peak Dennis narcissism - making women's bodily autonomy about his viewing pleasure

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: men should have some say in what women do with their hair? I mean, for Christ's sakes, we're the ones that have to look at it all day!

jokes.text: what we'd like you to do is kill the dog puppies, if you could. Hey. Don't-don't say 'kill,' though. Uh, sorry, murder them, if possible?
jokes.setup: They're trying to request a dog abortion professionally
jokes.punchline: Mac suggests 'murder' as a more appropriate word than 'kill'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac logic - trying to be more polite while suggesting a worse word

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: what we'd like you to do is kill the dog puppies, if you could.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Hey. Don't-don't say 'kill,' though. Uh, sorry, murder them, if possible?

jokes.text: Yes, hi. Yes, we need you to perform this dog abortion, and if you don't, my dog is gonna die. And he-he-he's not gonna be able to survive... Yes, I'm aware that a boy dog can't have puppies.
jokes.setup: Mac is on the phone trying to get veterinary help
jokes.punchline: The person on the phone points out the obvious biological impossibility
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag of Mac's gender confusion

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yes, hi. Yes, we need you to perform this dog abortion, and if you don't, my dog is gonna die. And he-he-he's not gonna be able to survive... Yes, I'm aware that a boy dog can't have puppies.

jokes.text: Look, there was a transition at some point, all right? He had a sex change. We see past gender at our bar.
jokes.setup: Mac is trying to explain his dog's pregnancy to the vet
jokes.punchline: He claims his dog had gender reassignment surgery
jokes.explanation: Mac's desperate attempt to maintain his delusion by inventing dog gender transition

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Look, there was a transition at some point, all right? He had a sex change. We see past gender at our bar.

jokes.text: Frankie. Take it easy, buddy. Your valve's getting all worked up. Hey, your valve's acting up... You know what? Here, here... eat some cat food.
jokes.setup: Frank is getting agitated about Dee's haircut plan
jokes.punchline: They treat his heart valve like a separate entity and feed him cat food as medicine
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie/Mac pseudoscience treating Frank's heart condition with pet food

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Frankie. Take it easy, buddy. Your valve's getting all worked up. Hey, your valve's acting up... You know what? Here, here... eat some cat food.

jokes.text: You seriously want to buy me a car as long as I don't cut my hair?
jokes.setup: Dennis is trying to stop Dee from getting her haircut
jokes.punchline: His bribe escalates to buying her a car
jokes.explanation: Shows how desperate Dennis is to control Dee's appearance

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You seriously want to buy me a car as long as I don't cut my hair?

jokes.text: I was like Samson. I had hair down to my butt crack. I looked like a Greek god. You look like a bridge troll.
jokes.setup: Dennis dramatically describes his former long hair
jokes.punchline: Dee immediately deflates his grandiose self-image
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling dynamic - Dennis's narcissistic memory vs. Dee's harsh reality

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I was like Samson. I had hair down to my butt crack. I looked like a Greek god.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You look like a bridge troll.

jokes.text: I needed the money and I read an ad for a toupee company that said they would give good money for real hair, so I bought in.
jokes.setup: Dennis explains why he cut his hair
jokes.punchline: His mythic Samson story was actually about quick cash for a toupee company
jokes.explanation: Deflates Dennis's grandiose story with mundane financial motivation

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I needed the money and I read an ad for a toupee company that said they would give good money for real hair, so I bought in.

jokes.text: There it is. There what is, Frank? That's my old hair. He's given it a good home.
jokes.setup: Frank brings Dee to see something important
jokes.punchline: It's a man wearing Frank's old hair as a toupee
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd visual payoff to Frank's hair story - he visits his hair like it's a pet

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: There it is.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: There what is, Frank?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: That's my old hair. He's given it a good home.

jokes.text: I come by time to time to check it out, see how it's doing. Don't-don't say anything, because I don't want to make it feel bad.
jokes.setup: Frank explains his relationship with his former hair
jokes.punchline: He treats the hair as if it has feelings that could be hurt
jokes.explanation: Frank's emotional attachment to his severed hair is perfectly deranged

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I come by time to time to check it out, see how it's doing. Don't-don't say anything, because I don't want to make it feel bad.

jokes.text: Bitch, you don't think I'm a doctor? I was a medic in Desert Storm. I did Doctors Without Borders, I saved mad lives. See, you racist. That's what the problem is.
jokes.setup: Mac questions if Z is really a doctor
jokes.punchline: Z gives an impressive medical resume then calls Mac racist
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection making Mac feel guilty for reasonable skepticism

dialogue.speaker: Z
dialogue.line: Bitch, you don't think I'm a doctor? I was a medic in Desert Storm. I did Doctors Without Borders, I saved mad lives. See, you racist. That's what the problem is.

jokes.text: Bitch, I'm just playing with you. I ain't no motherfucking doctor. Oh! Are you serious?
jokes.setup: Z has just given an impressive medical background
jokes.punchline: He immediately reveals he was lying the whole time
jokes.explanation: Double reversal that makes Mac and Charlie look foolish for believing him

dialogue.speaker: Z
dialogue.line: Bitch, I'm just playing with you. I ain't no motherfucking doctor.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh!

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Are you serious?

jokes.text: Poke around in one of them trash cans, try to find an Allen wrench.
jokes.setup: Z agrees to perform the dog abortion despite no medical training
jokes.punchline: His surgical tool of choice is a random Allen wrench from trash
jokes.explanation: Perfectly horrifying suggestion that shows how unqualified Z is

dialogue.line: Poke around in one of them trash cans, try to find an Allen wrench.

jokes.text: I was wondering why I didn't have to pop this dog's eye back in its socket. That dog has a very solid eye.
jokes.setup: Mac realizes this isn't actually his dog Poppins
jokes.punchline: His way of identifying his dog is by how often he has to reattach its eye
jokes.explanation: Gloriously grotesque detail about Poppins that's both specific and horrifying

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I was wondering why I didn't have to pop this dog's eye back in its socket. That dog has a very solid eye.

jokes.text: He came back to take care of his family. He did. He likes to take... Oh, no, he did not. He did not. There he goes. He wanted the blanket.
jokes.setup: Mac sentimentalizes Poppins returning to care for his offspring
jokes.punchline: Poppins immediately abandons them and steals their blanket
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Mac's romantic notion with immediate selfish dog behavior

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: He came back to take care of his family. He did. He likes to take... Oh, no, he did not. He did not. There he goes. He wanted the blanket.

jokes.text: Sometimes I find it very arousing, tears, but this time, I wasn't... I-I wasn't coming in-in that zone.
jokes.setup: Dennis enters the salon and sees a crying woman
jokes.punchline: He openly admits tears usually arouse him but not right now
jokes.explanation: Peak Dennis creepiness - admitting to being aroused by crying women

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Sometimes I find it very arousing, tears, but this time, I wasn't... I-I wasn't coming in-in that zone.

jokes.text: I thought you were gonna say that you only cut women's hair, and I was gonna claim discrimination to get you shut... it doesn't matter.
jokes.setup: Dennis is confused when the salon cuts men's hair
jokes.punchline: He admits his plan was to manufacture a discrimination lawsuit
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's manipulative nature and willingness to weaponize legal system

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I thought you were gonna say that you only cut women's hair, and I was gonna claim discrimination to get you shut... it doesn't matter.

jokes.text: From a genetic standpoint, I mean, her hair is just as much mine as it is hers, you know what I mean?
jokes.setup: Dennis tries to stop Dee's haircut appointment
jokes.punchline: He claims genetic ownership of his twin sister's hair
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Dennis logic extending twin genetics to hair ownership rights

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: From a genetic standpoint, I mean, her hair is just as much mine as it is hers, you know what I mean?

jokes.text: I'm crying because my dog is lost, asshole. What's your dog look like?
jokes.setup: Dennis assumes the worker is crying about her job destroying lives
jokes.punchline: She's crying about her lost dog, which Dennis might have information about
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Dennis realizing he might know about her dog

dialogue.speaker: Salon Worker
dialogue.line: I'm crying because my dog is lost, asshole.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What's your dog look like?

jokes.text: there's no doggy paternity test to actually prove that they were Poppy's puppies in the first place.
jokes.setup: Mac gave away Poppins's potential offspring
jokes.punchline: He justifies it by citing lack of dog DNA testing
jokes.explanation: Mac applies modern legal concepts to avoid dog parenting responsibility

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: there's no doggy paternity test to actually prove that they were Poppy's puppies in the first place.

jokes.text: I ended up having to do it all by myself! That's right! Listen, it looks... terrible. So bad. I mean, really, really bad.
jokes.setup: Dee returns having gotten her haircut
jokes.punchline: Even she admits her self-administered haircut looks terrible
jokes.explanation: Visual payoff to Dee's defiant haircut plan gone horribly wrong

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I ended up having to do it all by myself! That's right! Listen, it looks... terrible. So bad. I mean, really, really bad.

jokes.text: I've-I've forced my sister into a back-alley chop.
jokes.setup: Dennis sees the results of Dee's DIY haircut
jokes.punchline: He frames it as forcing her into illegal surgery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay connecting hair cutting to illegal abortion terminology

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I've-I've forced my sister into a back-alley chop.

jokes.text: I don't care about any of this, really... I don't care that much, either... Yeah, I guess I don't really care that much, either.
jokes.setup: They've spent the entire episode obsessing over women's haircuts
jokes.punchline: They all suddenly claim not to care at all
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing their complete lack of self-awareness. After an episode of caring intensely, they casually dismiss it all.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I don't care about any of this, really...

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I don't care that much, either...

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah, I guess I don't really care that much, either.

jokes.text: Why did you guys make such a big deal about it, then? Well, uh, you know, that's, like, a guy thing. 'cause we're used to being in charge
jokes.setup: The guys admit they don't actually care about women's haircuts
jokes.punchline: They casually admit it was just about maintaining male dominance
jokes.explanation: Casual admission of sexist motivations delivered as matter-of-fact explanation

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Why did you guys make such a big deal about it, then?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well, uh, you know, that's, like, a guy thing. 'cause we're used to being in charge

jokes.text: Nah, she ain't got to worry about that. I groomed that shit off a border collie.
jokes.setup: Z suggests Dee might get a call from the hair's original owner
jokes.punchline: He reveals the hair came from a dog, not a human
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy twist - Dee's wearing dog hair as a wig. Great economy and shock value.

dialogue.speaker: Z
dialogue.line: Nah, she ain't got to worry about that. I groomed that shit off a border collie.
